article_meta.title: News Law & the Courts Black Lives Matter Director Spent Millions in Donations on Homes, Shopping, Vacations, Indictment

reasoning: The model makes some reasonable calls but also has notable issues. It correctly identifies author-prose bias in some cases, but the fourth item fabricates a quote ('Significant focus is placed on...') that doesn't appear in the article text. The first flagged item is a defensible catch of author slant. The second item about Cullors is borderline - the author does note 'she was never charged criminally and has denied any wrongdoing' but the framing is still arguably spin. The third item about source attribution is questionable - the article does cite 'the group's 2020-2022 tax filings' which is a specific source. The fourth item uses fabricated text not from the article, which is a false positive. No attribution rule violations (no quoted text flagged). Some genuine bias instances were caught but the quality is mixed.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: No quoted or attributed source language was flagged. All flagged text comes from the author's own prose or is fabricated text (item 4). No attribution rule violations.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Item 1 (spending spree/slant) is a defensible catch. Item 2 (Cullors spending) is somewhat defensible as spin but the author did include her denial. Item 3 (source attribution) is questionable - tax filings are cited as source. Item 4 fabricates text that doesn't appear in the article, making it a clear false positive. Overall 1-2 items are questionable or wrong.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The article has several bias instances: 'personal indulgences' is a loaded word choice by the author; 'Donations began pouring into' is somewhat loaded; the overall structural choice to aggregate multiple BLM-related fraud cases creates a pattern narrative. The model caught some of these (the spending spree generalization) but missed 'personal indulgences' and the word 'murder' used for George Floyd's death (though this became common usage). Moderate misses.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Item 1 labeled as Slant is reasonable. Item 2 labeled as Spin is defensible. Item 3 as Omission of Source Attribution is questionable since a source is cited. Item 4's Bias by Story Choice and Placement label would be correct if the text existed in the article. Labels are generally in the right ballpark for the items that are valid.
